definition: a battery is a device that stores electricity and is used in equipment such as phones, tablets, torches, etc
example: the battery only lasts a few hours

definition: a deal is a particular agreement between a customer and a company, especially one which gives the customer some benefits
example: we’re offering a special deal if you buy before the end of the month

definition: if something folds, or if you fold it, it bends so that one part of it covers the other. If something folds out, then it bends the other way and becomes straighter
example: the chair will fold down flat

definition: if you navigate a computer screen, you successfully find the part of it that you need. If you navigate a website, you find the page or pages that you want to see
example: it’s really easy to navigate your way round the screen
the navigation menu is a great help

definition: a password is a secret list of letters and numbers that you have to put into a computer or phone in order to be able to start using it
example: these phones use a password, which is not so secure

definition: if you plug something in, you connect it to a supply of electricity or to another piece of equipment
example: can I plug my phone in here?
put the plug into the socket
don’t unplug my phone, it’s still charging
translation: отключать от сети, выключать из розетки

definition: a piece of equipment that is powerful can do its job very well or quickly or to a very great degree
example: the new camera is much more powerful than my old one
how much processing power does your laptop have?

definition: if a computer system is secure, it can only be used by people who have permission to use it so information can be kept secret. If your password is secure, it is very difficult for someone to guess what it is
example: these phones use a password, which is not so secure
personal details are securely stored on our servers in Germany
access to the site was protected by security software
the operating system had a bug and was highly insecure

definition: a speaker is a piece of equipment that sound comes out of, for example in a phone or a TV or a radio
example: the phone has a fairly large speaker built in on the back

definition: if you store something, you keep it somewhere when you are not using it
example: the phone can store up to 50 voicemails at a time
there’s not much storage space in the attic

definition: on a smartphone or tablet, if you swipe, you touch the screen and move your finger to the left, to the right, up, or down in order to make it display what you want
example: just swipe your finger across the screen to unlock the phone
a downward swipe will close the app

definition: if something tempts you, or if you are tempted by it, it makes you want to do it or have it, although you think you shouldn’t
example: can I tempt you to another piece of cake?
we received a very tempting offer for the house
I managed to resist the temptation to buy another ice cream

definition: something that is tiny is extremely small
example: this battery lasts a tiny bit longer than the other one

definition: if you touch something, you put part of your body on it. On a phone or tablet or computer screen, a touch system or a touch screen is one which works when you put your finger on the screen
example: it uses a touch ID system so you don’t need a password
touch the screen to start the program

definition: if you unlock a computer, tablet, or phone, you do something that proves you have permission to use the device, for example by entering your password
example: just swipe your finger across the screen to unlock the phone
the phone automatically locks itself after 10 minutes

definition: when you upgrade something, you get a better or more recent form of the same thing
example: I want to upgrade my phone to the latest version
do you need help to install the upgrade?

definition: a version of something is a form of it which is different from other forms
example: I want to upgrade my phone to the latest version

definition: voice recognition is the ability of a computer program to understand and react to human speech
example: voice recognition software has improved over the last few years

definition: on a camera, the zoom is the feature which lets the camera take more detailed pictures
example: the camera has an excellent digital zoom
can you zoom in on the cherry tree?

definition: bright colours are light and strong, not dark. If a room or house is bright, a lot of light comes in
example: she wears a lot of bright colours
brightly-coloured flowers

definition: a chain is a set of metal rings, joined together in a line. You can wear a small chain as a decoration, for example round your neck or wrist
example: she was wearing a delicate silver chain round her neck

definition: debt is the state of owing money to someone. A debt is an amount of money that you owe
example: I hate being in debt

definition: your heel is the back part of your foot. The heel of a shoe is the bottom of the shoe at the back. Women’s shoes with a very high heel are called high-heeled shoes, or high heels
example: my heel was rubbing on the shoe

definition: a hole is an empty space on the surface of something that goes all the way through
example: I drilled a hole through the side of the bath

definition: if you are keen, you are very enthusiastic. If you are keen on something, you like it a lot
example: how keen on shopping are you?

definition: the label on something you buy is the part that has the name of the brand and other information on it
example: he’ll only buy something if it’s got a designer label (made by a famous designer)
label your suitcase with your name

definition: your luggage is the bags and suitcases you take with you when you are travelling. Be careful: luggage is always singular. You cannot talk about luggages
example: I made sure I left plenty of space in my luggage

definition: an occasion is a time when a particular event happens. An occasion is also the event itself
example: the funeral was a solemn occasion

definition: an outfit is a set of clothes that you wear together at the same time
example: I have one outfit I only wear on special occasions

definition: something that is plain is simple, with no unnecessary parts or decoration
example: he wore a plain blue shirt
let me speak plainly: the answer is no

definition: a purchase is something you have just bought. Purchase is also the act of buying something
example: he showed us his recent purchase: a huge sofa
tickets can be purchased in advance

definition: if something is rare, it is not very common or it does not happen very often
example: the museum houses a number of rare and valuable exhibits
he used to be keen, but he rarely plays the piano now

definition: if you say that something is a rip-off, you mean that it is much more expensive than it should be because the people selling it are greedy or dishonest
example: I never buy designer clothes; they’re just a rip-off
we got ripped off by a taxi driver

definition: if someone looks scruffy, their clothes are not smart or clean and they look very untidy
example: he always looks scruffy
they were both rather scruffily dressed

definition: on a piece of clothing, stripes are lines that go from top to bottom and are a different colour from the areas on either side. If a piece of clothing is stripy, it has stripes in its pattern
example: a stripy pullover
their skirts had red and blue stripes
he wore a blue striped jacket

definition: a tag is a piece of paper or plastic that is fixed to something to give information about it
example: your jacket has still got the price tag on

definition: something that is thick is not thin
example: a pair of thick socks
the thickness of the walls was nearly one metre

definition: if your clothes are tight, they fit very close to your body
example: tight jeans
she wrapped the scarf tightly round her neck

definition: something which is trendy is very fashionable. If a person is trendy, they wear fashionable clothes and go to fashionable places
example: he looks quite trendy
this hairstyle is the latest trend

definition: if objects that are made of wood or stone are carved, they have patterns or pictures on them which someone has made by cutting into the surface
example: a beautiful carved wooden mask
visitors have carved their names in the wood
the carving represented a large tree

definition: clay is a kind of soil which becomes very hard when it is dried and heated. It is used to make bowls, pots, etc
example: people there often cook in clay pots

definition: if you consume something, you eat it or drink it. In economics, to consume goods or energy means to use them and then replace them with more
example: most people consume more meat than is healthy
energy consumption has been significantly reduced
the average consumer eats four chocolate bars a week

definition: in British English, something which is cute is small and attractive, in a slightly childish way. In American English, it can also mean someone is good-looking
example: what a cute little dog

definition: to display something or a set of things means to show them clearly so that people can see them
example: the program will display a drop-down menu
there were some lovely works of art on display (arranged for people to see)

definition: a doll is a small figure of a person. Some dolls are made as toys for children, and other dolls are painted and used as decoration
example: she was crying because she had lost her favourite doll

definition: if something happens or continues forever, it never stops but carries on happening or existing
example: it’ll last forever

definition: something that is genuine is not a copy but is really what it seems to be
example: this jacket is genuine leather

definition: glass is a solid material that lets light through it, and that is used to make windows, mirrors, and objects such as bottles and containers for drinking out of
example: put the jam in a clean glass jar

definition: something that is gorgeous looks beautiful or tastes delicious
example: you look gorgeous in that dress

definition: if something is handmade, it has been made by someone using their hands and simple tools, and not by machines
example: she sold handmade jewellery

definition: if you insult someone, you say something rude to them or about them
example: how dare you insult me!
refusing to shake hands was a deliberate insult
he sent me a rude and insulting email

definition: a mask is an object that covers your face and that has another face painted on it
example: I bought a hand-carved wooden mask in Tanzania

definition: a model is a smaller copy of a larger object
example: a plastic model of the Eiffel Tower

definition: a pot is a container that is used for cooking, storing food, or growing plants in
example: a large cooking pot with two handles

definition: to print something means to use a machine to put words or pictures onto paper or cloth, often when making many copies of something. Printed material, or print books, are made of paper, and contrast with electronic or digital material that you can only read on a computer screen
example: we’re going to print 40,000 copies of the dictionary
I’ve got the e-book, but I’d like to buy a print copy too
I’ve got the e-book, but I’d like to buy a print copy too
a printing and publishing company

definition: a rug is a thick piece of cloth that is used to cover part of the floor in a room
example: a beautiful Turkish rug

definition: silk is a fine, smooth cloth which is made from thread that is produced by an insect called a silkworm. It is usually expensive
example: a beautiful silk scarf

definition: straw is the dried stems of plants such as wheat. It is used for animals to sleep on, and also for making bags, hats, or furniture
example: a straw hat

definition: if you throw something away, you get rid of it because you do not want it or need it any more
example: my wife refuses to throw them away even though we don’t want them

definition: if you are ungrateful, you deliberately do not say thank you or show any thanks to someone who has done something kind to you
example: I don’t want to sound ungrateful, but I don’t want any presents

definition: if a person or animal is aggressive, they behave in a very determined, angry, or violent way towards people
example: you have to be quite aggressive when you are negotiating over the price
several people reacted aggressively to the announcement
he was accused of both physical and verbal aggression (being violent and also being deliberately rude and insulting)

definition: someone or something that is disciplined behaves or is organised in a very carefully controlled way
example: the display was well organised and disciplined
she lacks self-discipline (the ability to control her own behaviour properly)

definition: a frame is an object made of something rigid that goes around the edge of a picture, door, window etc and keeps the picture, door, or window in place
example: a carved wooden picture frame
a framed photograph of her grandchildren

definition: garbage is rubbish that you throw away. You can also refer to things that are useless or that have no value as garbage
example: don’t try and sell things that are broken or garbage

definition: to shoot or to shoot a gun means to make a bullet come out of it
example: someone turned it into a target and started shooting at it
six people died in the shooting

definition: a target is an object that you try and hit when you are shooting as a sport. A target is also a particular thing you are trying to achieve in your job
example: someone turned it into a target and started shooting at it

definition: if one thing turns into another thing, it changes completely and stops being what it was and becomes the other thing
example: someone turned it into a target and started shooting at it
